original_image_tags: Back of a yellowed, aged paper document. Two punched holes are visible at the top edge. The paper contains significant "bleed-through" or ghosting of typed text from the reverse side, appearing reversed and mostly illegible. There are multiple ink stamps and a faint red rectangular stamp. The overall appearance is that of the reverse side of a filed government document.

elements.description: A faded blue rectangular stamp located near the bottom center. It reads 'MAR 8 1951', 'U.S. DEPT. OF JUSTICE', 'FBI', and 'RECEIVED - MAIL ROOM'.

judge.reason: Description B adds specific, unreadable stamp text that Description A does not mention, but they do not contradict each other on the visual elements they both acknowledge.
